BKB Water Quality Analysis

Exploratory analysis of water quality trends, site variation, and environmental indicators (1989–2019).

1990s → 2010s DO

8.91 → 6.10

Project Overview

This project examines water quality measurements collected across multiple sites to determine whether aquatic conditions are changing over time. The analysis focuses on dissolved oxygen, water temperature, salinity, pH, visibility, and site-level differences.

Business Problem

Is water quality declining over time, and which measurable factors are most associated with low dissolved oxygen?

Dataset Summary

  • Records: 2,371
  • Columns: 17
  • Time span: 1989–2019 (with 5 invalid year entries excluded from trend analysis)
  • Mean dissolved oxygen: 6.65 mg/L
  • Mean water temperature: 18.06 °C
  • Mean pH: 7.17
  • Mean salinity: 0.72 ppt
  • Mean Secchi depth: 0.52 m

Key Findings

  • Average dissolved oxygen dropped from 8.91 mg/L in the 1990s to 6.10 mg/L in the 2010s.
  • Dissolved oxygen has its strongest negative relationship with water temperature (r = -0.51).
  • About 27.0% of observed dissolved oxygen readings are below 5 mg/L.
  • Site-level averages show meaningful differences in oxygen conditions.

Visualizations

Yearly trends in dissolved oxygen and water temperature.
Correlation heatmap of the key numeric variables.
Dissolved oxygen versus water temperature.
Average dissolved oxygen by site.
Distribution of dissolved oxygen by decade.
Missing values by column.

Problem Identification

The data shows a clear decline in dissolved oxygen over time, especially when comparing the 1990s and 2010s. This indicates that water conditions may be becoming less favorable for aquatic life.

Root Cause Analysis

  • Water temperature is inversely related to dissolved oxygen.
  • Later decades show lower oxygen readings than earlier decades.
  • Some sites consistently perform better than others, suggesting localized environmental differences.
  • Missing values in key monitoring fields indicate room for stronger data collection practices.

Recommendations

  • Increase monitoring during warm periods and at lower-oxygen sites.
  • Investigate local conditions that may reduce dissolved oxygen.
  • Improve completeness of monitoring records.
  • Track dissolved oxygen and temperature together over time.
  • Prioritize restoration or mitigation where low oxygen is persistent.

Conclusion

The analysis suggests that water quality has weakened over time, with dissolved oxygen declining and showing a strong inverse relationship with water temperature. The findings support a focused monitoring and management response.

Prepared for GitHub portfolio and presentation use.
